query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor flows around mid-2026:

**Dominant trend: Record foreign net SELLING**
- **H1 2026 (Jan–Jun): 148.32 trillion won** ($95B) net sold — a record first-half outflow
- **Through July 3: 157.3 trillion won** ($102.5B)
- **Through July 7: ~171.43 trillion won** (~$112.9B)

**Recent weekly/daily detail:**
- May–June combined: 92.89 trillion won net sold (accelerating as won weakened)
- **June 30: record single-day 7.76 trillion won** net sell
- **July 7: 2.93 trillion won** net sell — 13th straight session of selling; KOSPI dropped 4.91% (circuit breaker)
- **June 12 exception:** rare net BUY of ~1.7 trillion won on an >8% KOSPI surge

**The paradox:** KOSPI still rose ~101% in H1 and ~91.9% YTD despite the selling. Foreign ownership actually *rose* to 40.47% (from 36.65% in January) because price gains on held Samsung/SK Hynix shares outweighed the value sold. ~90% of selling was concentrated in those two chipmakers. Retail (~99–105T won) and institutions (~35T won) absorbed the outflows. Drivers: profit-taking/rebalancing, ~6% won depreciation, and a global chip/AI selloff.

The full report, structured data points, and sources have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Foreigners Dump $106.8 Billion of Kospi Stocks in First Half as Samsung Ownership Hits 16-Year Low
sources.snippet: Foreign investors were net sellers of 148.316 trillion won on the Kospi in the first half, concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix.

sources.title: Foreign investors' record Kospi sell-off may continue in second half as won weakens
sources.snippet: A record 148 trillion won ($95 billion) in net selling by foreign investors during the first half; June 30 record single-day 7.76 trillion won.

sources.title: Foreign investors may have more Korean stocks to sell
sources.snippet: Foreign investors sold 157.3 trillion won ($102.5 billion) of KOSPI shares through July 3; foreign ownership rose to 40.47%.

sources.title: Kospi Plunges 4.91% as Foreign Sell-Off Tops 171 Trillion; Circuit Breaker Triggered
sources.snippet: July 7 net selling 2.93 trillion won, 13th consecutive session; YTD cumulative ~171.43 trillion won; KOSPI closed 7,656.31 down 4.91%.

sources.title: Kospi, SK Hynix, Samsung Electronics: Why foreign investors are selling
sources.snippet: On Monday June 8, overseas investors unloaded a net 1.24 trillion won of Kospi-listed shares.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Return After a Month... Net Buying 1.7 Trillion Won in KOSPI
sources.snippet: On June 12, KOSPI surged more than 8%; foreign investors net bought 1.7 trillion won.

sources.title: Kospi selloff is rebalancing, not vote against South Korea: KRX CEO
sources.snippet: Korea Exchange CEO Jeong Eun-bo said the selloff reflects portfolio rebalancing after massive 2025/2026 gains, not lost confidence.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Dump 150 Trillion Won in KOSPI in First Half
sources.snippet: KOSPI closed at 8,476.48 on June 30; foreign investors dumped nearly 150 trillion won in the first half.

sources.title: Korean won falls nearly 6% against dollar this year amid foreign stock sell-off
sources.snippet: Korean won weakened nearly 6% against the dollar YTD, pressured by massive foreign selling of domestic stocks.
